name: Suspicious Outbound SMTP Connections
notes:
This rule detects potential exfiltration over SMTP protocol.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: frack113
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Outbound SMTP Connections
SIGMA Description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over an un-encrypted network protocol other than that of the existing command and control channel. The data may also be sent to an alternate network location from the main command and control server.


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following Access.ACL Permit
when the event matches Destination Port is any of [25 or 587 or 465 or 2525]
when the event matches Initiated (custom) is any of true

when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'thunderbird.exe' or LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'outlook.exe' or LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\program files\microsoft\exchange server\%' or (LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\program files\windowsapps\microsoft.windowscommunicationsapps%' and LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'hxtsr.exe') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Outbound SMTP Connections on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Access.ACL Permit
AND when the event matches Destination Port is any of [25 or 587 or 465 or 2525]
AND when the event matches Initiated (custom) is any of true

AND NOT when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'thunderbird.exe' or LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'outlook.exe' or LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\program files\microsoft\exchange server\%' or (LOWER("Process File File Path") LIKE '%\program files\windowsapps\microsoft.windowscommunicationsapps%' and LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'hxtsr.exe') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Outbound SMTP Connections
Event description: This rule detects potential exfiltration over SMTP protocol.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
